release_notes: Two Ready Messages log improvements that work the same on Windows and macOS. (1) The log now persists across app close and system restart within the same calendar day. Lines are appended to <user_data_dir>/ready_log.txt as plain text under a DATE: header; on launch, if the header's date matches today's local date the body is restored into the widget as cold (non-emphasized) entries, otherwise the file is deleted and the log starts empty. (2) A daily rollover timer fires every 60 seconds while the app is running; when the local calendar date changes (midnight, or DST adjustment, or a system clock change) the in-memory log and the persistent file are both wiped clean so a new day starts blank. Also shortens timestamps in the Ready log to HH:MM (no seconds): both outgoing and restored-from-disk entries use the short format, and incoming peer broadcasts are normalized to drop any seconds an older peer may have included. The connection diagnostic log and the peer-message wire envelope still use the existing now_str (with seconds) for debug precision; only the user-facing Ready Messages panel is shortened.
